title: Dependency-graph node
description: One node = one addressable surface in the system: an HTTP endpoint, a React component, a hook, a service function, a SQLAlchemy model, or a test fixture. Nodes are written by extractors and read by Claude before edits.

schema_version.description: Bump when the node format changes incompatibly. Hooks refuse to read mismatched versions and CLI validate fails — cheap insurance for future migrations.

id.description: Stable, location-independent identity. For endpoints: METHOD::/path/with/{params}. For components/hooks: package::ExportedName. For models: app::ClassName. Path/file moves do NOT change the id.

kind.description: The category that picks which dossier folder applies and which extractor owns it.

feature.description: Owning feature slug. Cross-references docs/flows/<feature>.md when present. Optional — extractors emit null when not yet attributed. It's the join key for human-level reasoning, so populate it as soon as a node has a stable home.

source.description: Where the symbol currently lives. Path is informational; node identity comes from `id` so renames don't orphan the file.

repo.description: Repo basename (matches a [repos.<key>].path basename in the project's project.toml).

path.description: Repo-relative path. Null if unresolvable (e.g. dynamic route).

line.description: Best-effort line of the definition; null if inspect could not resolve it.

signature.description: What callers see. For endpoints: HTTP method/path/auth/request/response. For components: prop types. For hooks: argument and return shape.

structural_hash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical signature + body shape. Changing this is the trigger that marks dossier text and dependents as 'needs review'. See DRIFT.md scenario 3.

depends_on.description: Outgoing edges: nodes this node calls/imports/queries. Authoritative — written once per regen by the extractor that owns this node. The reverse index is computed separately and lives at `nodes/_index/dependents.json`; do not store dependents on a node file. See dependents.schema.json.

external_consumers.description: Known consumers outside the static graph: shipped iOS app versions, third-party integrations, scheduled jobs, webhooks. The graph cannot prove these are still alive — they're a hint to slow down.

tests.description: Test nodes that exercise this one. Populated by the test extractor matching Playwright fixtures, request URLs, or imported symbols.

dossier.description: Path to the LLM-curated companion markdown (relative to depgraph/).

extractor.description: Which extractor wrote this file. Used to know what to re-run on regen.

warnings.description: Extractor-emitted warnings: ambiguous call sites, dynamic URL construction, unresolved symbols. These surface in the PreToolUse injection so I know the picture is incomplete.

via.description: How the dependency is realized. `string_url` is a fuzzy match — it means the extractor matched a literal URL to a route but couldn't import-link it; treat as advisory.

confidence.description: Static-import edges are 'exact'. Concatenated/template URLs are 'fuzzy'. Edges added by hand or by the LLM are 'inferred'.
